meaning: adj.提尔的,提尔人的,提尔文化的n.提尔人
examples: This special purple dye was called Tyrian purple, and it was the preferred color of emperors.
The rare and expensive dye for cloth, Tyrian purple, complemented another famous local product, fine linen.
He laid her on a couch spread with cloths of Tyrian dye, and called her his wife.
In order to establish their credentials within the Hellenic world the Carthaginians conflated the Greek hero, Heracles (better known as Hercules), with Melqart, his Tyrian counterpart.
